title: Run Orbit build rerun
goal: Let Samwise coordinate a fresh main-based Orbit build attempt that inherits prior lessons, enforces product and process gates, and closes with a hybrid retrospective.

steps.text: Load the active Orbit execution and planning artifacts, beginning with `Docs/Orbit/Execution/Orbit-Build-Rerun-Checklist.md`, `Docs/Orbit/Execution/Orbit-Product-Acceptance-Checklist.md`, the latest Orbit rerun-prep note, and the latest canonical retrospective artifacts.
Summarize the current attempt contract: first-slice scope, required participants, required evidence, required product gates, and the hybrid retrospective closeout rule.
Confirm the approved lane is bootstrapped, run baseline validation, and stop if the rerun prerequisites are not green.
Record the active participants for this attempt and the concrete evidence expected from each role before implementation begins.
Run the first checkpoint slice without broadening scope beyond the active Orbit MVP boundary.

Before any `review-ready` or `MVP candidate` language, require each active owner to red-pen their own deliverable at least three times, then complete the product acceptance checklist, record the interaction-quality review pass, and separate feature, product, process, and persona-fidelity confidence.
Run hybrid retrospective closeout: `fan-out` first, short `roundtable` second, then one canonical `Starfish` synthesis.
Update the rerun-prep artifact or create a dated successor note so the next attempt inherits approved lessons, rejected lessons, unresolved questions, and startup conditions.
Pause for AJ review before claiming rerun success, promoting process changes, or making any promotion decision back toward `main`.

acceptanceCriteria: The rerun starts from the active Orbit checklist and acceptance docs instead of thread memory alone
Required participants and evidence are explicit before implementation begins
The first checkpoint slice stays bounded to the active Orbit MVP scope
Each active owner completes and records at least three red-pen passes on their deliverable before done language is used
Product acceptance and interaction-quality review are recorded before any MVP-candidate language
Hybrid retrospective closeout is complete and evidence-backed
The resulting artifacts distinguish feature, product, process, and persona-fidelity outcomes
The next attempt inherits an explicit updated prep artifact instead of implicit carry-forward

verification.text: Confirm the current attempt contract names the first slice, active participants, evidence expectations, and required review gates
Confirm each active deliverable owner recorded at least three red-pen passes or an equivalent evidence-backed self-review trail
Confirm the product acceptance checklist and interaction-quality review artifact both exist before any MVP-candidate claim
Confirm the rerun-prep artifact was updated or superseded with carry-forward lessons and next attempt conditions
